      "risk": "Attacker can distinguish valid order IDs (403) from invalid ones (404), enabling enumeration of all order IDs in the system. On a public-facing endpoint this is a textbook Insecure Direct Object Reference.",

Never reveal existence to unauthorized callers.\n\n```typescript\nconst order = await db.orders.findById(orderId);\nif (!order || order.customerId !== userId) {\n  res.status(404).json({ error: \"Order not found\" });\n  return;\n}\n```",

      "title": "Missing Authentication Gate — Unauthenticated Requests Reach the Database",
      "risk": "req.user?.id is undefined when no auth token is present. The handler still hits the database, wastes resources, and relies on the implicit `customerId !== undefined` check for safety. Any future refactor (e.g., orders with a null customerId for guest checkouts) turns this into a full auth bypass.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "getOrder handler, top of function — no userId guard",
      "reproduction": "Send a request with no Authorization header. The handler queries the DB, then falls through to the 403 path. If any order has a nullable customerId, res.json(order) is returned to an anonymous caller.",
      "fix": "Add an explicit 401 check before any DB work:\n\n```typescript\nif (!userId) {\n  res.status(401).json({ error: \"Unauthorized\" });\n  return;\n}\n```",

      "title": "Unhandled Database Errors Crash the Process",
      "risk": "No try/catch around the async DB call. A connection timeout, query error, or malformed orderId (e.g., SQL/NoSQL injection payload) causes an unhandled promise rejection that either crashes the Node process or returns a raw stack trace to the caller, leaking internals.",
      "severity": "High",
